event: The Fall of the Rebel Angels (detail)
symbols: nude figure falling — the angel stripped of heavenly glory, descending into the abyss
arched vault above — the gateway of heaven from which the rebel is expelled
fiery glow — the burning judgment of God upon pride and rebellion
darkness surrounding — the outer darkness to which the rebellious angels are cast
description: A dark, atmospheric composition showing a nude figure seen from behind, seated or falling in a shadowy space. Above and behind, a large stone arch or vault glows with warm orange-red light, as if a furnace or fiery space lies beyond. The figure's skin is lit by this warm glow, creating strong contrasts between illuminated flesh and deep surrounding darkness. The atmosphere is cavernous and oppressive. The palette is limited to warm ambers, oranges, and deep blacks.
theological_description: This detail from the Fall of the Rebel Angels captures the moment of cosmic expulsion — 'How you have fallen from heaven, morning star, son of the dawn! You have been cast down to the earth' (Isaiah 14:12). The naked, falling figure has been stripped of angelic splendor, cast out of the presence of God. Jesus himself declared: 'I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven' (Luke 10:18). The fiery arch behind suggests both the heaven from which the rebel is expelled and the fire to which he descends — 'the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels' (Matthew 25:41). Jude records that 'the angels who did not keep their positions of authority but abandoned their proper dwelling — these he has kept in darkness, bound with everlasting chains for judgment on the great Day' (Jude 1:6). The theological lesson is clear: pride — 'I will make myself like the Most High' (Isaiah 14:14) — is the primordial sin that shatters even angelic perfection.

scripture_refs.reason: How you have fallen from heaven — the fall of Lucifer through pride
Jesus saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven
Angels who abandoned their dwelling kept in darkness for judgment
